# Figure description: скв_399.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/raw_graphics/Колонки_ скважин/скв_399.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is a **borehole/well log (каротажная диаграмма)** — specifically a geophysical well log chart showing multiple geophysical measurement curves plotted against depth. It appears to be a composite well log (комплексный каротаж) typical of Soviet-era exploration drilling, with what appears to be electrical resistivity, spontaneous potential (SP), and possibly gamma-ray or other geophysical curves plotted in parallel tracks. At the bottom there is also a **контрольные кривые** (control curves) section.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The log covers a single borehole identified in the title area at the top as **уч-к Тасты-Талды** (Tasty-Taldy prospect/site), which is a geographic locality in northern Kazakhstan consistent with the regional survey area. The log appears to cover a substantial depth interval — likely from near-surface down to several hundred meters (possibly 600–1200 m based on the length of the log and Soviet drilling practice for this type of survey). The header contains borehole metadata including what appears to be a well/скважина number, location coordinates, and survey parameters, though these are difficult to read at this resolution.

---

## Key Content

The figure contains **three to four parallel geophysical log curves** running the full length of the borehole, plotted against a depth scale on the left margin:

- **Left track**: Appears to show a relatively smooth curve, possibly **spontaneous potential (ПС — потенциал самопроизведения)** or natural gamma (ГК), which shows moderate, relatively low-amplitude fluctuations in the upper portion and larger deflections in the middle-lower section.

- **Central track(s)**: Show higher-amplitude, more jagged curves consistent with **electrical resistivity logs (КС — кажущееся сопротивление)** — likely gradient or potential electrode array measurements. These curves show significant variability throughout, with pronounced peaks and troughs suggesting alternating resistive and conductive lithological units.

- **Right track**: A third curve of similar character to the resistivity logs, possibly a **second resistivity measurement** (different electrode spacing) or an **induction log**, showing broadly correlated but distinct variations from the central track.

The overall pattern suggests **interbedded lithologies** — likely alternating harder/more resistive units (possibly carbonates, volcanics, or cemented sandstones) with softer/more conductive units (clays, shales, or saturated sands). There are several notable **anomalous intervals** mid-log where all curves show simultaneous strong deflections, which may indicate lithological boundaries, fluid-bearing horizons, or mineralised zones of exploration interest.

The **upper ~15–20% of the log** shows relatively subdued curve responses (possibly Quaternary or Neogene cover), while the **middle and lower portions** are much more variable (likely Paleozoic basement or older sedimentary/volcanic sequences).

At the very bottom, a separate shorter section labeled **КОНТРОЛЬНЫЕ КРИВЫЕ** (control curves) shows repeat measurements over a short interval — standard Soviet practice to verify log quality and tool calibration.

## Text and Labels

**Title area (top centre):**
> уч-к Тасты-Талды

**Header block (partially legible):**
- Contains fields for: скважина №, площадь (area), дата (date), масштаб (scale), and operator/геолог names
- Likely includes: Масштаб глубин (depth scale) and Масштаб кривых (curve scale)

**Column headers (top of log tracks, partially legible):**
> ПС (spontaneous potential)
> КС (apparent resistivity) — possibly with electrode array designations such as А2М0.5N or similar

**Curve legend/header block (upper right area):**
Contains technical parameters for each curve including electrode spacing, current strength, and measurement units — typical Soviet karotazh header format. Includes text approximating:
> Параметры каротажа
> Зонд / Масштаб / Единицы

**Mid-log annotation (upper section):**
> Поверхностная (??) — possibly **Поверхностный слой** or a formation name

**Lower section label:**
> КОНТРОЛЬНЫЕ КРИВЫЕ
> (followed by a sub-label, possibly) Глубина №...

**Right margin**: Contains numerical depth values and possibly lithological or stratigraphic interval annotations (barely legible at this resolution).

**Bottom right corner**: A small box likely containing the **draughtsman/technician signature block** and document number, consistent with Soviet technical drawing standards (штамп).

---

## Research Value

This well log provides direct subsurface geophysical data for the **Tasty-Taldy prospect**, allowing a geologist to correlate lithological units, identify potentially mineralised or fluid-bearing horizons based on resistivity anomalies, and calibrate surface geophysical interpretations (gravity, magnetic, seismic) against actual borehole conditions. The multiple resistivity curves enable **lithological discrimination** and assessment of whether anomalous intervals represent economic mineralisation targets (e.g., polymetallic sulphides, which show characteristically low resistivity). Combined with the regional 1:200,000 survey context (Report 36572), this log helps constrain the depth and character of basement structures and evaluate the mineral potential of the Tasty-Taldy area within the broader northern Kazakhstan geological framework.
